American businesswoman (born 1943)

|Judith R. Faulkner |
| --- | --- |
|Born |( 1943-08-11 ) 11 August 1943 (age 82) |
|Other names |Judy Faulkner |
|Alma mater |Dickinson College  
University of Wisconsin–Madison |
|Occupation |Healthcare software entrepreneur |

**Judith R. Faulkner** (born August 11, 1943) is an American billionaire businesswoman who is the CEO and founder of Epic Systems , a healthcare software company located in Verona, Wisconsin . <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> Faulkner founded Epic Systems in 1979, with the original name of Human Services Computing. <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> In 2013, _Forbes_ called her "the most powerful woman in healthcare", <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> and as of July 2024, estimated her net worth at US$7.8 billion. <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>

## Early life and education

 [edit ]

Faulkner was born on August 11, 1943, <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> to Louis and Del Greenfield. <sup>[[ 7 ]](,_2017-7)</sup> Faulkner's parents inspired her early interest in healthcare; her father, Louis, was a pharmacist, and her mother, Del, was the director of Oregon Physicians for Social Responsibility . <sup>[[ 7 ]](,_2017-7)</sup> She was raised in the Erlton neighborhood of Cherry Hill, New Jersey , and graduated from Moorestown Friends School in 1961. <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup> She received a bachelor's degree in mathematics from Dickinson College and a master's degree in computer science from the University of Wisconsin–Madison . <sup>[[ 7 ]](,_2017-7)</sup>

## Career

 [edit ]

While getting her master’s degree, Faulkner took a course on computing in medicine under Dr. Warner Slack. She was tasked with developing a system to keep track of patient information. <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup> In 1979, shortly after receiving her master's degree, Faulkner co-founded Human Services Computing, with Dr. John Greist. <sup>[[ 7 ]](,_2017-7)</sup> Human Services Computing, which later became Epic Systems , began in a basement at 2020 University Avenue in Madison, Wisconsin . <sup>[[ 7 ]](,_2017-7)</sup> The company was started with a $70,000 investment from friends and family but has never taken investment from venture capital or private equity and remains a privately held company . <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> In fact, Faulkner prides herself in the fact that Epic is homegrown; they have never acquired another company, <sup>[[ 7 ]](,_2017-7)</sup> <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup> and Faulkner has stated they will never go public. <sup>[[ 13 ]]()</sup> Epic Systems now holds the medical records of over 325 million people. <sup>[[ 14 ]]()</sup> Faulkner and her family currently own 43 percent of Epic Systems. <sup>[[ 15 ]]()</sup>

## Awards and recognition

 [edit ]

* _Forbes_ ' America's Top 50 Women In Tech 2018 <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>
* Forbes ranked her as the 55th most powerful woman in the world in 2023. <sup>[[ 16 ]]()</sup>

## Personal life

 [edit ]

Faulkner lives in Madison, Wisconsin . <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> She is married to Gordon Faulkner, a pediatrician, <sup>[[ 17 ]](.com-17)</sup> and they have three children. <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 17 ]](.com-17)</sup>

In 2015, Faulkner signed The Giving Pledge , committing 99% of her assets to philanthropy. <sup>[[ 18 ]]()</sup> In 2019 Judy and Gordon founded the Roots & Wings Foundation, <sup>[[ 19 ]]()</sup> which funds nonprofit organizations that serve low-income children and families.
